topic: Validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop: structural layout, bidirectional path-coupling repair, the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow, reference migration across commands/agents/READMEs/advisor-corpus, and whether fallback-router.ts should be wired for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback.

focus: Structural layout: enumerate directories, classify fold targets, verify mechanical safety
noveltyJustification: First broad pass; all structural findings new; most significant is correction that node_modules borrow is already resolved (117-research stale), changing merge risk profile.

ruledOut.approach: Resolve 8 system-spec-kit node_modules reach-ins as part of merge
ruledOut.reason: Already resolved: runtime ships own package.json with bare specifiers for zod/better-sqlite3/tsx; only tsc binary borrowed at dev-time

graphEvents.label: node_modules tooling-borrow is already resolved (bare specifiers)
system-spec-kit path coupling is depth-invariant to rename
How many bidirectional path-couplings break silently?

focus: Bidirectional path-coupling: enumerate requires + string literals, classify silent vs loud breakage
noveltyJustification: Bidirectional enumeration new; silent-vs-loud classification of 50 string literals in compile-command-contracts.cjs is genuine new risk signal not in spec prose.

ruledOut.approach: Direction A (14 requires) is the primary merge risk
ruledOut.reason: Requires break LOUDLY (MODULE_NOT_FOUND at load); the 50 silent string literals in Direction B are the real exposure

graphEvents.label: check-contract-drift.cjs is a built-in migration-completeness gate

focus: system-spec-kit tooling-borrow residual: depth-invariance, net simplification vs risk transfer, single-writer contract
noveltyJustification: Mixed new+confirmation. Depth-invariance proof and two-axis verdict (simplification on workflows<->runtime, neutral on system-spec-kit) are new; adds single-writer-contract insight (do NOT inline the seam).

ruledOut.approach: Inline review-research-paths.cjs into system-deep-loop during merge
ruledOut.reason: Intentional single-source-of-truth seam; duplicating reintroduces drift the re-export was designed to prevent

graphEvents.label: system-spec-kit coupling neutral to merge (3 depth-invariant intentional seams)
merge is net simplification: 15 cross-skill requires + 50 string literals become intra-skill

focus: Reference migration surface: 6 tiers ranked by loudness, advisor coupled-pair constraint, .claude/agents mirror trap
noveltyJustification: 6-tier loudness classification and .claude/agents mirror trap are new; MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID identity-propagation + coupled-pair atomic-migration constraint is genuine new risk. Refines Q4 with concrete mitigation.

ruledOut.approach: Migration is uniform across all surfaces
ruledOut.reason: Tiered: 2 automated guards (advisor drift-guard + check-contract-drift) gate routing/contract surfaces; prose in .claude/agents mirror + cross-skill docs is ungated

graphEvents.label: advisor MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID propagates to ~40 routing entries (coupled-pair migration)
.claude/agents mirror is silent-migration trap (5 files x 2 runtimes)

focus: fallback-router.ts wiring: zero callers, 4-piece wiring cost, approval-guard semantics, verdict preserve+track-followup
noveltyJustification: Mostly confirmation (zero callers) + structured 4-piece wiring-cost breakdown and approval-guard semantic analysis (router would correctly reject unapproved MiMo swap). Verdict (preserve, don't wire, track follow-up) is net-new decision input.

ruledOut.approach: Wire fallback-router as part of the merge
ruledOut.reason: Out of scope (read-only phase) + moving-target conflict surface + approval-guard would reject unapproved MiMo swaps anyway; preserve router, track post-merge follow-up

graphEvents.label: fallback-router zero callers confirmed (dormant but valid)
wiring is 4-piece feature: registry bridge + retry hook + re-dispatch + approval field
